channel.title: CPP on Reion's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in CPP on Reion's Blog

item.title: C++ PIMPL Pattern
item.description: PIMPL means pointer to implementation, or private implementation. It shows up a lot in C++ projects and libraries. The basic idea is simple: keep private data and methods out of the public interface. Put the class implementation details behind a separate pointer-owned class. This is useful when building a stable ABI for a C++ library interface, and for cutting down compile-time dependencies.
